Journal Mania

This is the Journal Mania project page. Here you can find details about the project, its features, and how it was developed.

Project Overview

Projekt: Design of a shop which specialises in journaling

Tools: figma (Wireframes, Prototype), Miro (UserFlows)

Platform: App

Background

To start my UX Designer career, I wanted to create the prototype for a shop. Since I have already experience with shops from my work as project manager and software developer, I felt this was a good way to test out everything I have learned.

I noticed that Journaling videos on YouTube had a big comeback at the end of last year (2025). Because of that I got into journaling. I researched and found, that there is no shop yet (early 2026), who specialises in tools and workshops around that topic. This is the starting point for this project.

Research

To collect different experiences of people who ventured into journaling, I decided to watch several YouTube videos and read several blog posts about the topic.

User Flows

I collected the data and created user flows as a result:

First I created the user flow, which showcases the basic decision making when a person starts to journal.

Now I created the specific user flow, for the features that I wanted to focus on for this project.

Features

Summary of the features and the reasoning behind them:

  • Feature: reason:
  • Feature: reason:
  • Feature: reason:

Conclusion

While working on the journaling shop, I learned that 'learning'. I am really glad, that I decided to create a user flow, since I came back to it every now and then. It helped me focusing on the important use cases.

I am also very pleased with the results of my work with Figma. Even though I use it in the free version, I already had the pleasure of using a lot of its features.

); }
